title: Magical Velvety Filipino Dried Shrimp Sinigang
description: A garlicky and adventurous Filipino dish featuring dried shrimp, nuka pickle, and a sugar free friendly twist on the classic sinigang recipe.
instructions: Rinse the dried shrimp and soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Drain and chop the dried shrimp into smaller pieces.
Slice the tomato and onion into thin pieces.
Mince the garlic and green chili.
Heat a pot over medium heat and saute the garlic, onion, and tomato.
Add the chopped dried shrimp, nuka pickle, fish sauce, and salt, and stir well.
Pour in the tamarind broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the spinach and stir well, cooking until the leaves are wilted.
Serve the sinigang hot, garnished with additional green chili if desired.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
